title: Understand: Reconstruction: The Thirteenth Amendment
description: Check your understanding of the Thirteenth Amendment with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: America’s Second Founding: Slavery, the Civil War, Emancipation, and Reconstruction

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Thirteenth Amendment extended and enshrined into the Constitution which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Emancipation Proclamation
The Black Codes
Jim Crow laws
The Reconstruction Acts

assessment_items.question_content:
**What did the Thirteenth Amendment do?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It banned slavery in the United States.
It gave Black people citizenship.
It extended the right to vote to Black men.
It created a formal pathway to reunification of the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Thirteenth Amendment bans involuntary servitude for all people except those who \_\_\_\_. **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: have committed a crime
were born before 1850
lived in the border states
were born into enslavement

assessment_items.question_content:
**The language in the Thirteenth Amendment mirrors the language of what other important document?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Northwest Ordinance
The Declaration of Independence
The Treaty of Paris
The Articles of Confederation

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the impact of the second section of the Thirteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It gave Congress the power to enforce the abolition of slavery.
It allowed involuntary servitude for convicted criminals.
It allowed border states to continue the institution of slavery.
It incorporated the rights in the first ten amendments to apply to the states.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was one objection the Democrats in Congress had to the Thirteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Confederate states were not represented in Congress when the amendment was framed and approved by Congress.
The Constitution already abolished slavery.
There was no way Congress could enforce the Thirteenth Amendment.
The criminal exemption was too broad and could lead to slavery again.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the result of the criminal exemption of the Thirteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The use of convict labor in the United States
The loss of the right to vote for former convicts
The suspension of the rights of those accused of crimes
The passage of the Fourteenth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is true about the criminal exemption clause of the Thirteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The language was taken from similar clauses in state constitutions in the North.
It has been largely ignored by Congress and has not been a subject of debate.
It has been overturned and ruled unconstitutional by the Supreme Court.
The criminal exemption clause applies only to prisons in the American South.

assessment_items.question_content:
While Republicans in Congress were drafting the Thirteenth Amendment, they were divided on how broad the Amendment should be. 

**Which statement best describes one key division?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: One group believed the amendment should only abolish slavery; the other group believed the amendment should offer an explicit promise of equality.
One group believed the amendment should apply only to Confederate states; the other group believed the amendment should apply to all states.
One group believed the amendment should end only the transport of newly enslaved people; the other group believed the amendment should end all forms of enslavement.
One group believed the amendment should apply only to enslaved people over the age of eighteen; the other group believed the amendment should apply to all enslaved people.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which historical event most directly influenced Congress’s decision to approve the Thirteenth Amendment? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The completion of Sherman's March to the Sea
The assassination of Abraham Lincoln
The implementation Black Codes in the South
The collapse of Reconstruction

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which Reconstruction figure was concerned about the inclusion of the criminal exception in the Thirteenth Amendment? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Abraham Lincoln
Andrew Johnson
Charles Sumner
William T. Sherman

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement accurately describes how the United States treated the practice of chattel slavery from 1783 to 1860?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There were several federal measures that declared that owning enslaved people was the right of all American citizens.
There were no federal measures banning slavery, but most states banned slavery in their constitutions.
There was a single federal measure legalizing slavery, but it applied only to the southern states.
There were no federal measures banning slavery, and each state got to choose whether to allow slavery.
